name: Wanli UFO Village
description: A rare pod of derelict midcentury Futuro and Venturo houses lines a semi-abandoned beachfront resort outside Taipei.

name: Rainbow Family Village
description: Grandpa Rainbow felt the neighborhood needed some color, so he picked up a paintbrush and went to work.

name: Houtong Cat Village
description: Once a prosperous mining village, this tiny Taiwanese town is now overrun with cute cats.

name: The Dome of Light
description: The world’s largest work of glass art hides in this stunning Taiwanese subway station.

name: Taroko Gorge
description: An extraordinary natural landscape of mountains, waterfalls, and rocky cliffs riddled with tunnels.

name: Modern Toilet Restaurant
description: Looking for a crappy restaurant? Pull up a porcelain bowl and enjoy your meal.

name: Tuned Mass Damper of Taipei 101
description: Enormous pendulum helps keep Taiwan's tallest building from swaying.

name: Eternal Spring Shrine
description: A waterfall runs right through the middle of this Taiwanese shrine to the hundreds of workers killed while building a nearby highway.

name: Yehliu Geopark
description: This Taiwanese promontory is covered in otherworldly hoodoo formations.

name: Lotus Lake Taoist Complex
description: This collection of lakeside pagodas and temples are flanked by giant lions, tigers, and dragons as well as their welcoming maws.

name: Wind City Moon Restaurant
description: A nostalgic restaurant evokes life in Taiwan from the 1940s to the 1970s.

name: Carton King Creative Park
description: Life-size animals and furniture made entirely from cardboard fill this odd paper-themed restaurant.

name: Fufudingshan Shell and Coral Temple
description: A Buddhist temple covered in intricate designs and carvings made out of seashells and pieces of coral.

name: Formosan Aboriginal Culture Village
description: This attraction is part anthropological journey and part amusement park.

name: The High Heel Church
description: Visit this church that's shaped like a giant glass pump either to cater to women or to reference a tragic local tale. Or both.

name: Anping Tree House
description: The tangled roots of a giant banyan tree have fully reclaimed the ruins of this abandoned warehouse.

name: Gulu Gulu
description: Paiwanese restaurant run by native indigenous Taiwanese singer songwriter, and home to a DIY arts-and-crafts folk garden.

name: Rixing Type Foundry
description: Taiwan's last Chinese character letterpress preserves the dying art of Chinese character mold making.

name: 100-Year Bomb Shelter
description: A secret passageway from World War II is now a quirky pedestrian shortcut.

name: Grand Hotel Tunnel
description: Air-raid tunnels hidden beneath this opulent hotel provided shelter and an escape route—and one of the world's longest slides.

name: Painted Animation Lane
description: A small alleyway lined with locally drawn scenes of nostalgic anime and cartoon characters.

name: Leaning Mailboxes
description: These charming mailboxes became social media stars after a typhoon failed to topple them.

name: Grand Matsu Temple
description: Only through deep darkness and tragedy did this elaborate royal palace become a sacred temple.

name: Treasure Hill
description: This densely-packed cluster of buildings was saved from the wrecking ball and turned into an art community.

name: Robert Y Museum
description: This former factory turned private museum contains surreal and creepy artwork created by Robert Y.

name: Bei Tou Incinerator
description: A waste incinerator with a restaurant on its chimney.

name: Jhaishan Tunnel
description: The wartime boat shelter now doubles as an underground concert hall where musicians play on rafts along the waterway.

name: Chef Hung Beef Noodle Soup
description: Taiwan’s iconic dish is in peak form here.

name: MeiNong I-Chan
description: Hakka-style teahouse where everybody grinds their own tea.

name: Luoshan Organic Tofu Farm
description: Learn to make this village's specialty: volcanic mud tofu.

name: Baguashan Skywalk
description: The longest skywalk in Taiwan.

name: Yanshui Beehive Fireworks Festival
description: A festival where people vie to be bombarded by exploding clusters of fireworks.

name: Yanshui Beehive Fireworks Festival
description: A festival where people vie to be bombarded by exploding clusters of fireworks.

name: Pure Love Diner Bar
description: This kitschy, kinky homage to Taipei’s old “grandpa shops” is full of eye-candy.

name: Green Island Human Rights Culture Park
description: A memorial to the thousands of political dissidents imprisoned during the 40-year period of martial law in Taiwan.

name: Miniatures Museum of Taiwan
description: The first miniatures museum in Asia contains all sorts of itsy-bitsy wonders, including the world's smallest working television.

name: Pagoda of the Tree Spirit
description: A monument to placate the spirits of an ancient forest.

name: The Imperial Japanese Navy Fengshan Wireless Communications Station
description: This former military installation was turned into a discipline center by the Taiwanese Navy.

name: Sun Yat-Sen Memorial Hall
description: Memorial to the father of the Republic of China.

name: Kaohsiung National Stadium
description: This dragon-shaped sports arena is the world’s largest solar-powered stadium.

name: Jinjiang Tea Room
description: Dine on Hakka dishes at a dreamy cottage nestled in the heart of Taipei.

name: Pier2 Art Center
description: A thriving community of Taiwanese art.

name: Ocean Fantasy Museum
description: An antiquated aquarium housed in a converted government anti-nuclear bunker.

name: 'Shoaf Kepl Poets'
description: A plaque by the roadside describes a three-dimensional glossary of an imagined language where words are numbers and numbers are words.

name: Real Guts Café
description: Movie buffs will love this Taiwanese director’s ode to film history.

name: Youzihu
description: The ruins of a prehistoric village hide on a remote Taiwanese island.

name: Ghost Lore Museum
description: A museum about rituals, ghosts, and demons from around Asia, set in the middle of the peaceful Jiufen.

name: Saba Milkfish Museum
description: A small museum dedicated to one of the most popular fish in Taiwanese cuisine.

name: Cijin Tunnel
description: A Japanese-era military tunnel is now a psychedelic art exhibit.

name: Talmud Business Hotel
description: A hotel in Taiwan based on the better business practices that can be gleaned from Jewish law.

name: Sanshing Green Onion Culture Palace
description: Eat green onion ice cream and other allium-centric treats at this unusual museum.

name: Buddha's Tooth Relic
description: At a museum dedicated to the millenia-old rites and art of Buddhism, the Buddha's reputed tooth is held as an object of worship.

name: Arkansas Diner
description: Down-home Arkansas comfort food comes with a side of American pop culture kitsch.

name: Xiziwan Tunnel (Old Shoushan Cave)
description: A 100-year-old Japanese command center is now a unique underground university walkway with periodic exhibits.

name: 'Rising of the Ghelyrns'
description: In a park on Taiwan's rugged Eastern coast, a marker celebrates a glorious sight from the fictional Kcymaerxthaere.

name: Shinhama Ekimae
description: Housed in a Japanese colonial-era bank, this cafe pours next-level cold brew.

name: Jimmy's Moon Bus
description: Step into this whimsical art installation based on a popular picture book.

name: Liu's Bakery
description: Try Taiwan's iconic Apple Bread—which traditionally contains no apples—at the bakery that invented it.

name: Xiaonanmen Fuzhou Fool’s Noodles
description: Diners have been slurping 'fool's noodles' at this shop for more than 70 years.

name: Alishan Sacred Tree Relics
description: You can still see the remains of a sacred 3,000-year-old cypress.

name: Beitou Folk Arts Museum
description: A Japanese colonial-era hot springs hotel that has transformed into an art museum.

name: Emmanuel Fresh Fish Soup
description: This third-generation shop has been doling out bowls of tilapia soup since 1977.

name: Guomao Community
description: High rises built to house displaced military personnel and their families form a unique cylindrical shape.

name: Carp Hill Miniatures
description: This hiking trail features an aging but endearing collection of folk miniatures depicting Taiwan’s landmarks.

name: Tainan Confucius Temple
description: The first Confucian temple in Taiwan, founded by the son of a pirate lord, doubled as the island's first state-sponsored school.

name: Puppetry Art Center of Taipei
description: A museum of puppets and puppetry covering all Asian styles.

name: Elephant Mountain
description: This hike brings visitors closer to Taipei 101, once the world’s tallest building, than any other trail.

name: Tianliao Moon World
description: Visit Taiwan’s badlands, which allows visitors to feel like they’re walking on the moon.

name: Madou Daitian Temple
description: Enter this dragon-shaped temple to see animatronic scenes depicting heaven and hell.

name: Fongshan Old City
description: Taiwan's first stone fortress is also the best preserved Qing Dynasty-era city on the island.

name: Garden of the Generalissimos
description: A Taiwanese garden teems with the retired statues of the island's former dictator.

name: National Human Rights Museum
description: This abandoned prison is now a museum chronicling the oppression of political prisoners in the not-so-distant past.

name: Snail Alley
description: The residence of a Taiwanese literary scholar sits down an alleyway adorned with depictions of snails.

name: Jiji Wuchang Temple
description: This lopsided, earthquake-damaged temple stands as a testament to Taiwan’s resilience.

name: Hunglodei Nanshan Fude Temple
description: A temple featuring a giant Tudigong statue, quirky roadside dinosaurs, and stunning Taipei panoramas.

name: Wufeng Mushroom Museum
description: An unassuming museum offers a deep dive into everything about mushrooms, from cultivation innovations to fungi that "talk."

name: Taipei Zebra
description: This crosswalk hosts one of Taipei’s most beloved public artworks.

name: Guanyin Cave
description: Hidden in a cave on a small Taiwanese island, a stalagmite reported to look like the bodhisattva Guanyin.

name: Confucius Rock
description: On Green Island, a rock resembling the ancient Chinese sage gazes contemplatively out at sea.

name: 921 Earthquake Museum
description: A once-shattered school now preserves a visible fault line, showcasing Taiwan's traumatic 1999 earthquake.

name: Suehiro Shrine at Hayashi Department Store
description: On the roof of the oldest department store in Tainan stands a Shinto shrine dating back to the 1930s.

name: Hexing Coal Mine
description: A glimpse into Taiwan's coal-mining past.

name: Sea Gazing Room
description: This public artwork, hidden down a cliffside hiking trail, encourages visitors to contemplate the tides.

name: Takao Railway Museum
description: A museum commemorates what was once the largest freight station in Taiwan.

name: Mausoleum of Chiang Kai-shek
description: The final resting place of the dictator of Taiwan.

name: ‘Mountain Home’
description: A forgotten hiker’s lodge, now reclaimed by the forest, with its chimney standing as a lone sentinel of the past.

name: Xianjiyan: Rock of the Deity’s Footprint
description: A rock, a temple, and a window into Taiwan’s rich cultural mythology—wrapped in a serene city escape.

name: Cijin Shell Gallery
description: This seaside museum boasts a colorful collection of over 3,000 shells, the largest in Taiwan.

name: Beishan Broadcasting Wall
description: These large speakers once broadcasted propaganda across the strait to the People’s Republic of China.

name: Tomb of the Netherlander
description: Behind a 300-year-old temple hides a small tomb said to house the bones of 17th-century Dutch soldiers.

name: Shoyoen
description: This unique blend of Japanese and Western architecture provides a glimpse into the lives of the Japanese elites who once ruled Taiwan.

name: Haiqiang Kindergarten Slide
description: No children ride down this antique concrete slide.

name: Sandiaoling Bike Tunnel
description: A century-old rail tunnel turned eco-friendly cycling path.

name: Keelung Tower
description: The bright-orange, crane-shaped tower offers panoramic views and showcases the city’s maritime identity.

name: Hongmaogang Baoan Temple
description: A rare Japanese-style temple and a Shinzo Abe Statue, honoring both spirits of the past and modern connections between Taiwan and Japan.

name: National Taiwan Museum Bronze Oxen
description: These two 90-year-old statues were once part of a Japanese Shinto shrine.

name: Songboling Visitor Centre
description: Vibrant tribute to Taiwan’s tea culture, blending tradition and innovation to create an unforgettable experience.

name: Wulixue Irrigation Canal
description: A preserved segment of the old irrigation canal recalls Taipei’s history as a “Water City.”

name: Green Island Lighthouse
description: An American lighthouse stands on an island cliffside in Taiwan.

name: Remains of the Takao Foreigners' Cemetery
description: Just a few graves remain in this neighborhood that was once a cemetery for foreigners.

name: Sperm Whale Exhibition Hall
description: Nestled between the Sicao Green Tunnel and Dazhong Temple, an antiquated exhibition hall boasts the complete skeletons of mother and child sperm whales.

name: Yellow Tiger Flag
description: The official flag of the short-lived Republic of Formosa.

name: Taipei Bridge Scooter Waterfall
description: The cascade created by Taipei’s rush-hour traffic has become one of the city’s quirkiest photo spots.

name: Manneken Pis of Taiwan
description: A replica of Brussels’ Manneken Pis has stood in Taiwan since 1934.

name: Danei Totoro Bus Stop
description: When Ghibli came to a Taiwanese village.

name: Taiwan Stock Museum
description: Learn all about Taiwanese financial history from inside a former high-security vault.

name: Yuanshan Water Shrine
description: A Japanese-era Shinto shrine next to Taipei's most famous night market.

name: Xingfu Village Monument
description: A century-old monument to commemorate a crowd-funded public cemetery.

name: The British Consulate at Takow
description: This historic 19th-century building was the center of British trade and diplomacy in Taiwan.

name: Fu Garden
description: A Greek-inspired memorial garden to commemorate a former university president and a liberal influential intellectual

name: Immortal Cave
description: You can hear Buddhist prayers in this elevated sea cave once used by monks.

name: Lanyang Museum
description: With a design inspired by the local cuesta rock formations, this museum serves as a gateway to Yilan’s history and geography.

name: Landing Spot of Dr. Mackay
description: This statue of a medical missionary is sometimes used to gauge flood levels during typhoons.

name: The Tiger Inscription
description: A 19th-century stone carving welcomes hikers on a historical trail between mountain and sea.

name: Chiang Statues in Beitou
description: An otherwise typical Beitou alley is home to statues of Chiang Kai-shek and his son.

name: NTU Giant Sequoia
description: A giant sequoia tree slice that traveled across oceans and lived through wars

name: Cho Myeong-ha Incident Monument
description: A trilingual monument marks the site of a Korean independence activist's assassination attempt on a Japanese prince in Taiwan.

name: Dr. Chen Wen-chen Memorial
description: A memorial honoring a mathematician likely murdered by Taiwan's authoritarian government.

name: Sino-French War Memorial Park
description: Keelung’s century-old French military cemetery.

name: Wu Feng Temple
description: A shrine for a once-beloved historic figure, whose mythology has been discredited.

name: Taiwan Oil Field Exhibition Hall
description: Where Taiwan found its oil...

name: Iûnn Tshing-khe Plane Crash Memorial
description: A tiny airplane-shaped monument marks the story of Taiwan’s first civilian pilot and his fatal crash in 1934.

name: Teresa Teng Statue
description: A statue of the global pop legend in a residential metro plaza.

name: Ghost Mask Museum
description: One man's hand-sculpted collection of colorful masks representing a host of gruesome spirits.

name: Funny Sex
description: Taiwan's only erotically-themed restaurant brings the comedy back to sex and dinner.

name: Tengfeng Fish Ball Museum
description: If you've ever wanted to learn about the history of Asian fish balls, this Taiwanese museum has you covered.
